commit.message:
Fix: MauiView.DidUpdateFocus only clears IsFocused if it was the one that set it (#34673)

## Description

`MauiView.DidUpdateFocus` was unconditionally setting `IsFocused =
false` whenever native iOS focus moved away from the view. This was too
aggressive — it would overwrite any `IsFocused = true` value that had
been set by a third-party vendor directly toggling the property.

## Changes

Added a `_isFocusedSetByUs` boolean field to `MauiView`. In
`DidUpdateFocus`:
- When this view gains focus (`context.NextFocusedView == this`), the
flag is set to `true` alongside setting `IsFocused = true`.
- When focus moves away, `IsFocused` is only set to `false` if
`_isFocusedSetByUs` is `true` (i.e. this code was the one that set it).
The flag is then reset to `false`.

This ensures we don't obliterate a third-party vendor that is
independently toggling `IsFocused` on the cross-platform view.

## Related

Fixes the behavior described at:  

https://github.com/dotnet/maui/blob/638865c51c8b5dc0cf5ce30629a65c2ff197b602/src/Core/src/Platform/iOS/MauiView.cs#L799-L812

commit.message:
Phase 3: NodeRejected ClusterEvent and NodeRejectionReason (#143)

* feat: add NodeRejected ClusterEvent and NodeRejectionReason

Phase 3 of the version-compatibility implementation plan.

Extends ClusterEvent with a NodeRejected variant emitted when a node
fails the version handshake or the handshake transport call fails.

cluster.rs:
- ClusterEvent::NodeRejected { node_id, reason, detail }
- NodeRejectionReason enum: IncompatibleProtocol, IncompatibleAdapter,
  ConnectionFailed
- NodeRejectionReason is separate from handshake-level RejectionReason
  to avoid coupling cluster events to wire protocol internals
- From<RejectionReason> for NodeRejectionReason conversion
- Display impl for NodeRejectionReason
- Serde derives (conditional on serde feature)
- 6 unit tests: construction, emitter delivery, From conversion,
  Display, ConnectionFailed variant, equality

lib.rs:
- Re-export NodeRejectionReason

Design decisions:
- NodeRejectionReason lives in cluster.rs (not system_actors.rs) to
  keep cluster events independent from wire protocol types
- ConnectionFailed is cluster-level only (not in handshake proto) since
  it represents local transport failure, not a remote rejection
- From<RejectionReason> bridges the handshake domain to cluster domain

Zero clippy warnings across full workspace. All workspace tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address multi-model review findings for Phase 3

- Add #[non_exhaustive] to ClusterEvent and NodeRejectionReason enums
  (ChatGPT+Gemini: adding NodeRejected is a breaking change for
  exhaustive match; non_exhaustive makes future extensions safe)
- Update ClusterEvents::subscribe doc to mention NodeRejected
  (Haiku: doc only listed NodeJoined/NodeLeft)

ProtocolError variant (Sonnet) declined: the design doc section
referenced describes post-handshake system message failures, which
is a separate concern from Phase 3 scope. Can be added when that
error-handling scenario is implemented.

feat: log WebSocket frames by default, -ws-no-log to suppress

After a WebSocket upgrade (101 Switching Protocols), httputil's bidirectional
copy bypasses ModifyResponse entirely. To give operators visibility into WS
traffic without buffering or payload modification, frame headers are now parsed
and logged as they stream through the proxy.

Implementation:
- wsLoggingTransport: wraps the HTTP transport; intercepts 101 responses and
  replaces resp.Body with a wsLoggingConn
- wsLoggingConn: stateful io.ReadWriteCloser wrapper that parses WS frame
  headers in both directions with zero buffering (bytes pass through unchanged)
- wsFrameParseState: per-direction header accumulator; handles arbitrary chunk
  sizes across Read/Write calls; supports short (≤125 B), 16-bit, and 64-bit
  payload lengths, masked frames, control frames, and fragmented frames
- Logger.LogWSFrame: logs opcode name, conn ID, direction, payload length,
  masked and fragment flags; no-op when logWS=false

Log output example:
  WS↕  conn#1 established
  WS↑  conn#1  op=text    len=42 [masked]
  WS↓  conn#1  op=text    len=38
  WS↓  conn#1  op=ping    len=0

Flags:
  (default)    WS frames logged to stderr
  -ws-no-log   suppress WS frame logging

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(framework): create shadow root for components with pre-existing slot content (#221) * fix(framework): create shadow root for components with pre-existing slot content When a shadow-DOM component (meta.sd=1) has child nodes already present at connectedCallback time, \() must still create a shadow root. Previously, the condition: else if (this.childNodes.length > 0) { root = this; isSSR = true; } treated ALL elements with children as SSR light-DOM content, skipping shadow root creation entirely. This is wrong for shadow-DOM components whose children are slot content — not SSR output. This bug surfaces during SPA partial rendering: the server sends back a parent template containing child custom elements with their projected content already inlined: Reply The browser parses this into an with childNodes. When the element upgrades, \ sees children and assumes SSR hydration, leaving the element without a shadow root. The button renders unstyled and its template (with ) is never applied. The fix checks meta.sd (explicit shadow-DOM declaration in the compiled template) before falling into the SSR light-DOM path: else if (this.childNodes.length > 0 && !meta.sd) { … } When meta.sd is set, the element proceeds to the wantShadow branch which calls attachShadow() and populates from the template. Existing children remain in the light DOM and project through as intended. When meta.sd is NOT set (e.g. components using global __webui_shadow fallback), children are still treated as SSR content — preserving the existing light-DOM hydration path for the shadow-via-global-flag case. Includes a Playwright regression test (slot-shadow fixture) covering: 1. Empty child — baseline, always worked 2. Child with pre-existing slot content (static HTML) 3. Dynamically spawned child with slot content (programmatic) Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> * Potential fix for pull request finding 'Unused variable, import, function or class' Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <223894421+github-code-quality[bot]@users.noreply.github.com> * fix test --------- Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <223894421+github-code-quality[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix DISPLAY_SIZE_CHANGED feedback loop causing spam and framerate drop

UIParent:SetScale() fires DISPLAY_SIZE_CHANGED synchronously, which was
re-triggering ApplyScale() in an infinite loop.

Fix:
- Check self._applying in the event handler and bail early
- Hold _applying=true for 0.1s via C_Timer so it covers the deferred event
- Skip ApplyScale in timer callback if scale is already correct

commit.message:
`clang-tidy`: resolve `bugprone-macro-parentheses` (#500)

Warnings were confined to two header files and determined to be false
positives—parentheses around uses of parameters in C++ code generation
macros are not only superfluous, but disastrous for successful
compilation.

Macros are now protected from this check _en bloc_ due to the
impossibility of threading perl-line `NOLINT` statements in macro
definitions with line continuations.

commit.message:
fix(security): upgrade axios to 1.15.0 - CVE-2026-40175, CVE-2025-62718 (#966)

Critical S360 action items for SFI-ES5.2 1ES Open Source Vulnerabilities.

CVE-2026-40175 (CVSS 9.9): Unrestricted Cloud Metadata Exfiltration
via Header Injection Chain — prototype pollution gadget enables CRLF
injection in HTTP headers, bypassing AWS IMDSv2 session tokens.

CVE-2025-62718: NO_PROXY Bypass via Hostname Normalization — trailing
dots and IPv6 literals skip NO_PROXY matching, enabling SSRF through
attacker-controlled proxy.

Upgraded in 3 packages:
- extensions/copilot: 1.14.0 → 1.15.0
- extensions/cursor:  1.13.5 → 1.15.0
- agent-os-vscode:    1.13.6 → 1.15.0
